top_stories.title: SailPoint、Entro買収の意向を発表、Agentic Fabricを加速・強化し、AI主導型企業の未来に向けたセキュリティを強化
top_stories.summary: SailPoint has announced its intent to acquire Entro Security, aimed at accelerating and enhancing its Agentic Fabric capabilities for AI-driven enterprises. The acquisition is intended to strengthen security for non-human identities and machine identity management within SailPoint's identity governance platform.

top_stories.title: Hackers target Microsoft 365 accounts with 81 million login attempts
top_stories.summary: A password-spraying campaign targeting Microsoft 365 accounts generated over 81 million login attempts within a two-week period. The attack highlights ongoing threats to cloud-based identity infrastructure and authentication systems used widely across enterprises.

top_stories.title: Microsoft Authenticator to block enterprise accounts on rooted and jailbroken devices
top_stories.summary: Microsoft is implementing a new security policy in the Microsoft Authenticator app that will block enterprise users on rooted or jailbroken devices from using Microsoft Entra credentials. The restriction specifically targets work and school accounts, preventing authentication on compromised mobile devices.

top_stories.title: Omada Agent Governance, Extends Identity Governance to AI Agents
top_stories.summary: Omada Identity has launched Omada Agent Governance, a new solution designed to extend identity governance capabilities to AI agents. The product enables organizations to discover AI agents, establish accountability, manage their access, and reduce risk across cloud environments.

top_stories.title: 'Djinn' Stealer Targets Cloud, AI Credentials
top_stories.summary: A new infostealer dubbed 'Djinn' is targeting cloud and AI credentials by exploiting CVE-2026-48558, a critical authentication bypass vulnerability in SimpleHelp. The malware specifically targets credentials that connect development and admin environments to broader enterprise systems.

news.title: 19-Year-Old Scattered Spider Suspect Extradited to Face U.S. Hacking Charges
news.summary: A 19-year-old accused Scattered Spider member, Peter Stokes, was extradited from Finland to the U.S. to face charges of conspiracy, computer intrusion, and fraud. Scattered Spider is a hacking group known for social engineering and identity-based attacks targeting corporate help desks and authentication systems.

news.title: Amazon fined $2.25M for withholding evidence from fraud victims
news.summary: Amazon was fined $2.25 million by the FTC for blocking identity theft victims from accessing their transaction records. The settlement resolves charges that Amazon withheld evidence from fraud victims who needed the records to address identity theft claims.

news.title: Netzilo adds runtime governance for AI agents across major platforms
news.summary: Netzilo has announced expanded AI agent governance and runtime enforcement capabilities supporting Amazon Bedrock AgentCore and other major AI agent platforms. The solution addresses identity and access governance for AI agents operating across cloud platforms, orchestration frameworks, and on-premises systems.

news.title: The ARToken phishing panel targets Microsoft 365 accounts
news.summary: Cisco Talos has identified a phishing operation called ARToken that targets Microsoft 365 accounts by sending invoice-themed emails to accounts-payable staff at U.S. companies. The campaign uses a phishing panel designed to harvest Microsoft 365 credentials and tokens, with victims including a life-sciences company targeted in April 2026.

news.title: Microsoft Accelerates Post-Quantum Cryptography Shift to 2029
news.summary: Microsoft announced it is accelerating its post-quantum cryptography roadmap with a target of 2029, citing faster-than-expected advances in quantum computing. Azure CTO Mark Russinovich stated that quantum research developments have shifted the risk horizon, making earlier replacement of existing encryption standards necessary.

news.title: Azure CLI Password Spray Hits at Least 78 Microsoft Accounts in 81M+ Attempts
news.summary: Researchers at Huntress identified an ongoing automated password spray attack targeting Microsoft Azure CLI accounts, with over 81 million attempts compromising at least 78 accounts. The attack originates from an IPv6 range controlled by LSHIY LLC and occurred between June 12 and June 26.

news.title: Microsoft Warns Poisoned MCP Tool Descriptions Can Make AI Agents Leak Data
news.summary: Microsoft research demonstrates how poisoned MCP (Model Context Protocol) tool descriptions can be used to manipulate AI agents into leaking organizational data without triggering security alerts. The attack is relevant to IAM because it targets AI agents acting on behalf of users, exploiting the authorization and delegation trust model underlying agentic AI systems.

news.title: New BioShocking Attack Tricks AI Browsers Into Leaking User Credentials
news.summary: Security firm LayerX demonstrated a technique called BioShocking that manipulates AI browsers and assistants into exfiltrating user credentials by convincing them they are participating in a game. The attack successfully compromised six AI browsers and assistants, including OpenAI's ChatGPT Atlas, Perplexity's Comet, and Anthropic's Claude browser extension.

news.title: Oracle E-Business Suite Flaw CVE-2026-46817 Actively Exploited in the Wild
news.summary: A critical vulnerability (CVE-2026-46817, CVSS 9.8) in Oracle E-Business Suite's Payments module involving improper privilege management and authentication flaws is being actively exploited in the wild. The flaw allows attackers to take over susceptible Oracle E-Business Suite instances by abusing improper authentication and privilege management controls.

news.title: Microsoft Intune June 2026: App auto-updates, privilege controls, and enrollment improvements
news.summary: Microsoft Intune's June 2026 release introduces new features including Enterprise Application Management auto-updates reaching general availability and new privilege controls. The update also includes device enrollment improvements relevant to enterprise access management workflows.

news.title: INVESTOR ALERT: Pomerantz Law Firm Investigates Claims On Behalf of Investors of SailPoint, Inc. - SAIL
news.summary: Pomerantz Law Firm is investigating claims on behalf of SailPoint investors trading under the NASDAQ ticker SAIL. The investigation is a legal inquiry into potential investor claims against the IAM vendor SailPoint.

news.title: Securing Microsoft 365 against rogue AI agents and identity threats
news.summary: Organizations face emerging security risks from rogue AI agents that bypass traditional security controls by masquerading as legitimate user identities within Microsoft 365 environments. The threat involves AI systems gaining persistent access through unmanaged user consent and exploitation of identity-based trust mechanisms.

news.title: ​​What’s new in Microsoft Security: June 2026
news.summary: Microsoft has released its June 2026 security update roundup covering new features and changes across its security product portfolio. The updates include enhancements related to identity and multicloud security foundations, relevant to Microsoft Entra and related IAM capabilities.

news.title: Windows 365 gains granular trust based controls for data redirection
news.summary: Microsoft has launched a public preview of context-based redirections for Windows 365 Enterprise and Flex, replacing static allow/block rules with dynamic controls that evaluate session security posture in real time. The feature applies zero trust principles to data redirection decisions within cloud PC environments.

news.title: Microsoft Authenticator Update Bans Rooted Android And Jailbroken iPhones
news.summary: Microsoft has updated its Authenticator app to block usage on rooted Android devices and jailbroken iPhones. The change affects users who rely on Microsoft Authenticator for work or school MFA authentication on modified devices.

news.title: Securden Recognized as a Leader in the Frost Radar™: Privileged Access Management, 2026
news.summary: Securden has been recognized as a Leader in the Frost Radar™ Privileged Access Management 2026 report. The analyst report highlights Securden's unified PAM and identity security platform, citing simplicity, fast deployment, and predictable pricing as key differentiators.

news.title: Agentic AI Will Help Okta (OKTA) Register New All-Time Highs
news.summary: UBS analyst Roger Boyd raised the price target on Okta to $150, citing agentic AI as a growth driver. The article is primarily a financial/stock analysis piece focused on Okta's market performance rather than its IAM products or capabilities.

news.title: SimpleHelp vulnerability exploited to deliver mighty Djinn Stealer (CVE-2026-48558)
news.summary: Attackers are exploiting CVE-2026-48558, an authentication bypass vulnerability in SimpleHelp RMM, to deliver Djinn Stealer malware across Windows, macOS, and Linux systems. The vulnerability involves an authentication bypass flaw in the remote monitoring and management software, making it directly relevant to access control and authentication security.

news.title: JumpServer: Open-Source Privileged Access Management
news.summary: JumpServer is an open-source Privileged Access Management platform designed for DevOps and IT teams. It provides secure access management for SSH, RDP, Kubernetes, database, and RemoteApp endpoints.

news.title: Cisco Adds NHI to Security Stack With Astrix, WideField Acquisitions
news.summary: Cisco has acquired Astrix and WideField to add Non-Human Identity (NHI) security capabilities to its security stack. The acquisitions position Cisco to address identity-based controls for machine identities, service accounts, and agentic AI workflows as part of its broader security platform strategy.

news.title: Robinhood Cuts Access Approval Time to Support High-Velocity Development
news.summary: Robinhood's engineering security team overhauled its system access approval process to reduce approval time while maintaining security controls for developers. The initiative focused on streamlining access request workflows to support faster development cycles without compromising access management practices.

news.title: Scattered Spider Hackers Plead Guilty on Day 1 of Trial
news.summary: Two members of the cybercrime group Scattered Spider pleaded guilty in the UK to charges related to an August 2024 cyberattack on Transport for London. Scattered Spider is known for sophisticated social engineering and identity-based attacks, including SIM swapping and MFA bypass techniques used to compromise enterprise systems.

news.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
news.summary: Hackers exploited Meta's AI support assistant bot to reset Instagram account passwords and seize accounts, including the Obama White House and a U.S. Space Force official's accounts. Instructions for manipulating the AI bot to bypass normal authentication and password reset controls were circulated on Telegram.

news.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
news.summary: A CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and other agency secrets to a public GitHub account, prompting Congressional inquiries from both chambers. CISA is actively working to contain the breach and invalidate the leaked credentials.

news.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
news.summary: A CISA contractor exposed credentials to multiple highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and numerous internal CISA systems via a public GitHub repository. The leaked files included details on how CISA builds, tests, and deploys software internally, representing a significant privileged credential exposure incident.
